motivation: Complete the K/V outlier sample by adding Meta Llama family. Llama 3.1 8B is the mainstream 8B-class reference; Llama 3.2 3B is a smaller companion to check family consistency. Both confirm Meta's training recipe produces uniformly controlled outlier distributions.

llama_3_1_8b_instruct.note: Reproducibility verified: second run with fresh Colab session produced identical K=8.81, V=4.32, L0 K=3.09.

findings.meta_recipe_signature: Both Llama models exhibit unusually clean outlier profiles: V_max 4.32-4.74x (lowest in our 11-model sample), K concentrated at late layers (25-29), no Layer 0 pathology. Meta's training recipe appears exceptionally effective at controlling activation outliers.
findings.family_consistency: Both 3B and 8B share the same qualitative pattern (low V across all layers, K peaking at second-to-last layer). The 3B's slightly higher Layer 0 K (5.84 vs 3.09) may reflect capacity-limited compression of representations, but remains far below any threshold of concern.
findings.gqa_ratio_irrelevance: Llama 3.1 8B (GQA 4:1) and 3.2 3B (GQA 3:1) show similar outlier profiles despite ratio difference. Combined with Qwen2-7B's catastrophic 7:1 ratio and Qwen2.5-14B's safe 5:1 ratio, GQA ratio does not predict pathology -- training recipe dominates.

pathology_rate: 1 of 11 models (~9%) exhibits the K@Layer0 >= 15x pathological pattern. The single case (Qwen2-7B) is an older Alibaba training recipe; the successor Qwen2.5-14B has no pathology. All other vendors (Meta Llama x2, Google Gemma x3, Microsoft Phi x2, Mistral, DeepSeek) pass cleanly.
v_outlier_tolerance_confirmed_up_to: 39.63x (Gemma 4 31B Layer 12, NIAH 21/21). V outliers do not disrupt attention structure regardless of magnitude in this range.
